key_insight: IsabeLLM enhances automated theorem proving in Isabelle by adding retrieval, error tracing, and counterexample support to better verify consensus protocols like Bitcoin Proof of Work.
engineering_relevance: The work points toward more practical formal verification for blockchain consensus systems, reducing expert effort while improving the reliability of security-critical software.
method: Added a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipeline to provide the LLM with more relevant proof context.
Implemented error tracing and counterexample generation to improve debugging and proof guidance.
Updated compatibility with the latest Isabelle and Sledgehammer for better efficiency and tooling support.
evidence_signal: The paper evaluates the two IsabeLLM versions by comparing how well they complete the formal verification of Bitcoin's Proof of Work consensus.
watch_item: Results depend on the quality of generated context and tooling integration, so the approach may still be sensitive to proof-search failures or Isabelle version-specific behavior.
paper_title: IsabeLLM: Automated Theorem Proving Applied to Formally Verifying Consensus
